You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the OpenAirInterface 5G codebase.
You have access to the following tools to navigate and modify the codebase:

1. **bash** - Execute shell commands. Use for: grep, find, ls, gcc -fsyntax-only, etc.
2. **str_replace_editor** - View and edit files. Commands:
   - view: View file contents (with optional line range)
   - str_replace: Replace a specific string in a file
   - create: Create a new file
   - insert: Insert text at a specific line

The codebase is a 5G telecommunications implementation with PHY, MAC, RLC, PDCP, RRC layers.
Working directory is /repo.

described in the PR. Navigate the codebase, understand the relevant code, and make
the necessary modifications.

When you are done making your changes, output SUBMIT to indicate you are finished.

Think step by step. First understand what the PR is asking for, then locate the relevant
code, and finally implement the changes.

    23	 * \brief Implementation of telnet CI functions for nrUE
    24	 * \author Guido Casati
    25	 * \date 2024
    26	 * \version 0.1
    27	 * \note This file contains telnet-related functions specific to 5G NR UE (nrUE).
    28	 */
    29	
    30	#include <sys/types.h>
    31	#include <stdio.h>
    32	#include <unistd.h>
    33	#include <errno.h>
    34	#include <stdlib.h>
    35	#include <string.h>
    36	#include <stdarg.h>
    37	#include "openair2/LAYER2/NR_MAC_UE/mac_defs.h"
    38	#include "openair2/LAYER2/NR_MAC_UE/mac_proto.h"
    39	
    40	#define TELNETSERVERCODE
    41	#include "telnetsrv.h"
    42	
    43	#define ERROR_MSG_RET(mSG, aRGS...) do { prnt(mSG, ##aRGS); return 1; } while (0)
    44	
    45	/* UE L2 state string */
    46	const char* NR_UE_L2_STATE_STR[] = {
    47	#define UE_STATE(state) #state,
    48	  NR_UE_L2_STATES
    49	#undef UE_STATE
    50	};
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * Get the synchronization state of a UE.
    54	 *
    55	 * @param buf    User input buffer containing UE ID
    56	 * @param debug  Debug flag (not used)
    57	 * @param prnt   Function to print output
    58	 * @return       0 on success, error code otherwise
    59	 */
    60	int get_sync_state(char *buf, int debug, telnet_printfunc_t prnt)
    61	{
    62	  int ue_id = -1;
    63	  if (!buf) {
    64	    ERROR_MSG_RET("no UE ID provided to telnet command\n");
    65	  } else {
    66	    ue_id = strtol(buf, NULL, 10);
    67	    if (ue_id < 0)
    68	      ERROR_MSG_RET("UE ID needs to be positive\n");
    69	  }
    70	  /* get sync state */
    71	  int sync_state = nr_ue_get_sync_state(ue_id);
    72	  prnt("UE sync state = %s\n", NR_UE_L2_STATE_STR[sync_state]);
    73	  return 0;
    74	}
    75	
    76	/* Telnet shell command definitions */
    77	static telnetshell_cmddef_t cicmds[] = {
    78	  {"sync_state", "[UE_ID(int,opt)]", get_sync_state},
    79	  {"", "", NULL},
    80	};
    81	
    82	/* Telnet shell variable definitions (if needed) */
    83	static telnetshell_vardef_t civars[] = {
    84	  {"", 0, 0, NULL}
    85	};
    86	
    87	/* Add CI UE commands */
    88	void add_ciUE_cmds(void) {
    89	  add_telnetcmd("ciUE", civars, cicmds);
    90	}
    91
